claim: Fifth measurement. 20-gene balanced panel with the full Session-6 stack: Rust backend (cell_sim_rust wheel built and installed), 4-worker parallel, 5-gene calibration, extended pool set now including TOTAL_EVENTS (cumulative event count) in addition to non-metabolic pools. Scale=0.05, t_end=0.5. Result: MCC=0.229 (same as v2, v3). TOTAL_EVENTS noise floor at 5 calibration samples is ~0.30 so its threshold is too loose to trip on non-catastrophic KOs. Overall wall per gene effective: 1.9 s (vs 12.7 s in v2) - Rust+parallel gives ~7x speedup. Detection ceiling is architectural: only pgi-class central-glycolysis KOs trip at any tested short-window config.
caveats: MCC identical to v1/v2/v3 across scale={0.05, 0.10, 0.25} with threshold calibration and expanded pools. The short-window detection floor is fundamental to simulator behaviour, not detector tuning.
TOTAL_EVENTS noise floor requires MORE calibration samples to shrink - at 5 it's 30%%, at 20+ probably <10%%, making it a useful signal. Worth re-running with calibrate=20 in a future session.

The brief's MCC>0.59 target is not reachable with 0.5 s bio-time runs. It requires either (a) t_end on the order of tens-to-hundreds of seconds bio-time (many minutes of wall time per gene even with Rust, infeasible in an interactive session), or (b) a per-rule event-count detector that treats 'events for catalysis:X dropped to zero in KO of gene catalysing X' as the signal.

Infrastructure wins this session are real even though MCC didn't move: Rust path built and verified (2x speedup), parallel sweep validated (4x speedup), non-metabolic pool framework in place, TOTAL_EVENTS signal live.
